fieldValues.value: RFC-022: Relational lifecycle states — requiresRelation + transition fulfillment
ext:lifecycle (LifecycleState, transition semantics), lifecycle.json, type.json, canonical CLI contract (record transition), repo validate diagnostics

Supersession is a compound semantic act — flip the predecessor to `superseded`, create a successor, assert a `supersedes` relation — that the model previously could not couple, so a bare state flip produced orphan supersessions: records marked superseded with no successor and no link (live example: the gallery decision 38501856, since healed). RFC-022 introduces relational states: a `LifecycleState` may declare `requiresRelation` ({relationType: string|string[], direction: incoming|outgoing default incoming}), meaning a Record may only occupy that state if a satisfying Relation exists (state ⇒ relation; the converse direction stays valid, preserving the two-phase drafted-successor workflow). Transitions into such a state are hard-rejected unless already satisfied, or fulfilled atomically through the existing transition operation (`fulfillment.newRecord` | `fulfillment.existingInstanceId`); no new verb is introduced. Allowed-transitions projections expose the obligation structurally, ending state-name string-matching in clients; repository validation surfaces at-rest violations as warning diagnostics. Conformance rules R1–R10 in the proposal artifact; spec invariants I-88, I-89, I-90. Alternatives considered and rejected: transition-level spawnsSuccessor marker (door-dependent invariant), a first-class supersede verb (forks the single write path), advisory-only enforcement (preserves the failure mode); pure relational derivation of superseded-class states is recorded as the long-term direction.
